channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Software supply chain attacks: check your dependencies
item.description: Attackers are compromising open-source packages to spread malware. Cyber defenders are asked to review dependencies to reduce risks

item.title: How courts are coping with a flood of AI-generated lawsuits
item.description: Most days in her chambers, Judge Maritza Braswell, a federal magistrate judge in Colorado, sifts through stacks of documents written by people without a lawyer. Many of them can’t afford to hire a lawyer, and others have cases too weak or too small to interest one. She reads each one carefully,...

item.title: Over 1.4 Million Accounts Disrupted in Cybercrime Crackdown
item.description: Law enforcement and tech companies disrupted infrastructure linked to scammers operating across Southeast Asia. The post Over 1.4 Million Accounts Disrupted in Cybercrime Crackdown appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Fake Sites Mimicking Open-Source Tools Rank High on Google to Deliver Malware via TDS
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a large-scale operation that impersonates open-source and freeware projects to funnel unsuspecting users through a Traffic Distribution System (TDS) and deliver malware families like Remus Stealer, AnimateClipper, and the SessionGate framework. "The sites are...

item.title: Hackers Spied on a Stock Exchange Executive's Outlook Mailbox for Five Months
item.description: Unknown attackers spent at least five months inside the Outlook mailbox of a senior executive at a major global stock exchange, copying the inbox out in small, repeated batches and routing it through Dropbox and OneDrive so the traffic blended into normal cloud activity. Symantec and Carbon Black's...

item.title: Cloudflare Analytics API latency out of AMS
item.description: Jun 4, 09:27 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 4, 09:08 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating latency issues with Cloudflare Analytics API, specifically affecting the Amsterdam (AMS) region. These issues do not affect other API services nor the serving of cached files...

item.title: Cisco Warns of Available PoC for Critical Unified CM Vulnerability
item.description: The high-severity flaw can be exploited remotely, without authentication, in server-side request forgery (SSRF) attacks. The post Cisco Warns of Available PoC for Critical Unified CM Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: VS Code Vulnerability Allows One-Click GitHub Token Theft
item.description: A researcher has disclosed the full details of the vulnerability and released a PoC without notifying Microsoft in advance. The post VS Code Vulnerability Allows One-Click GitHub Token Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: CISA Adds Exploited Magento RCE Flaw CVE-2026-45247 to KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a critical flaw impacting Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento full-page cache extension, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitation in the wild. The...

item.title: Network Congestion in Frankfurt
item.description: Jun 4, 06:59 UTC Resolved - Cloudflare observed network congestion in Frankfurt from 05:53 UTC to 06:08 UTC. The issue is now resolved.

item.title: DoJ Disrupts Southeast Asia Crypto Fraud Networks, Freezes $3.8 Million in Assets
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Wednesday announced the results of a sweeping action undertaken by government authorities and private sector companies to combat cyber-enabled and cryptocurrency fraud targeting Americans. The "Disruption Week" operation began May 18, 2026, leading to the...

item.title: Toward Pre-Deployment Assurance for Enterprise AI Agents: Ontology-Grounded Simulation and Trust Certification
item.description: arXiv:2606.04037v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Pre-deployment verification of enterprise artificial intelligence (AI) agents remains a critical gap between large language model (LLM) capability benchmarking and production deployment. Post-deployment monitoring, human-in-the-loop controls, and...

item.title: Stumbling Into AI Emotional Dependence: How Routine AI Interactions Reshape Human Connection
item.description: arXiv:2606.04150v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Public discourse and emerging policy typically assume that AI emotional support is a deliberate act: a lonely user consciously seeking comfort from a dedicated companion chatbot. In this paper, we draw on emerging empirical evidence and argue that...

item.title: Thinking Through Signs: PEEL as a Semiotic Scaffolding for Epistemically Accountable AI-Enabled Research
item.description: arXiv:2606.04152v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are reshaping research practice while quietly eroding researchers epistemic accountability. This commentary introduces PEEL - Protocols for Epistemically Engaged Literacy in AI, a working scaffolding that combines deterministic...

item.title: SMAC-Talk: A Natural Language Extension of the StarCraft Multi-Agent Challenge for Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.04202v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As LLMs become more widely deployed, they are increasingly expected to work alongside other AI agents rather than operating in isolation. Effective coordination in these settings requires agents to communicate, share information and make decisions...

item.title: Consensus is Strategically Insufficient: Reasoning-Trace Disagreement as a Knowledge-Representation Signal
item.description: arXiv:2606.04223v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-agent systems are commonly designed to reduce disagreement through voting, consensus protocols, debate, or fault-tolerant aggregation. We argue that this objective is insufficient for value-laden tasks, where disagreement may reflect genuine...

item.title: Early Detection of Alzheimer's Disease Using Explainable Machine Learning on Clinical Biomarkers: A Multi-Class Classification Study Using the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) Dataset
item.description: arXiv:2606.03995v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Background: Alzheimer's disease (AD) affects over 55 million people worldwide. Accurate, interpretable detection of normal cognition (NC), mild cognitive impairment (MCI), and AD from routine clinical assessments remains a critical unmet need....

item.title: Novel Aspects of IEEE SA P3109 Arithmetic Formats for Machine Learning
item.description: arXiv:2606.04028v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The IEEE P3109 draft standard defines a parameterized family of binary floating-point formats and associated operations, with a focus on facilitating machine learning. These formats allow efficient and consistent representation of values in a small...

item.title: Position: Deployed Reinforcement Learning should be Continual
item.description: arXiv:2606.04029v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has received increasing attention and adoption in real-world use cases. Most of these systems follow a train-then-fix paradigm, where trained agents do not learn while interacting with the world until performance degrades...

item.title: Pseudospectral Bounds for Transient Amplification in Coupled Gradient Descent
item.description: arXiv:2606.04031v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Coupled gradient descent--where the update of one parameter block depends on another--underlies bilevel optimization, two-time-scale stochastic approximation, and adversarial training. When the coupled Jacobian is block-triangular, asymptotic...

item.title: Do Transformers Need Three Projections? Systematic Study of QKV Variants
item.description: arXiv:2606.04032v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transformers have become the standard solution for various AI tasks, with the query, key, and value (QKV) attention formulation playing a central role. However, the individual contribution of these three projections and the impact of omitting some...

item.title: POLARIS: Guiding Small Models to Write Long Stories
item.description: arXiv:2606.04095v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Small open-weight models struggle at long-form creative writing: their generated stories either fall far short of the requested length, or their quality significantly degrades as length increases, especially when compared to frontier models. We...

item.title: Discourse-Role Labels as Presentation-Time Variables for Context Use in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.04109v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Context-augmented language model systems often wrap supplied content with labels such as Reference:, Evidence:, Instruction:, Note:, or Example:, but the effect of these labels on reader-model behavior remains underexplored. We introduce a paired...

item.title: Computational conceptual history of scientific concepts: From early digital methods to LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2606.04118v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This article situates large language models (LLMs) within the longer history of computational approaches to concept analysis in the history, philosophy, and sociology of science (HPSS). We examine what LLMs add to existing methods, how they inherit...

item.title: SaliMory: Orchestrating Cognitive Memory for Conversational Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.04120v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Conversational agents that serve as lifelong companions must maintain persistent memory across all interactions. However, simply expanding context windows with raw retrieval degrades reasoning quality, while training memory agents via standard...

item.title: When Retrieval Doesn't Help: A Large-Scale Study of Biomedical RAG
item.description: arXiv:2606.04127v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Medical question answering is a high-stakes setting where factual errors can have serious consequences. Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is widely viewed as a promising solution, and prior work has reported substantial gains for large medical QA...

item.title: [AINews] Reve 2 and Ideogram 4: Layouts in Imagegen
item.description: a quiet day.

item.title: Increased latency for Codex compaction for a subset of users
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe have identified that users are experiencing elevated latency for the impacted services. We are working on implementing a mitigation.Affected components CLI (Degraded performance) App (Degraded performance) VS Code extension (Degraded performance) Codex API (Degraded...

item.title: USTC Open-Sources Agent-Driven Long-Context Training Paradigm: 30B Matches Qwen3-235B
item.description: Researchers at the University of Science and Technology of China (USTC) have open-sourced a novel agent-driven long-context training paradigm that achieves breakthrough efficiency — a 30-billion-parameter model matching the performance of Alibaba'...

item.title: Alibaba's Qwen Opens to Third-Party Agents and Skills; KFC, Luckin, MIXUE Among First Adopters
item.description: Alibaba's flagship AI assistant, Qwen (also known as Tongyi Qianwen), has taken a major step toward becoming an open platform by opening up to third-party Agents and Skills. The move marks a significant shift in China's AI assistant landscape, pos...

item.title: 2026 Zhiyuan Conference to Explore Brain-Inspired Intelligence and Next-Generation AI
item.description: For seven years running, the Zhiyuan Conference has stood as one of artificial intelligence's most academically uncompromising gatherings. Organized by the Beijing Academy of Artificial Intelligence (BAAI), this annual event has drawn participatio...

item.title: Chinese Embodied AI Company Tops RoboArena Benchmark, Beating NVIDIA and Physical Intelligence
item.description: **Announced at NVIDIA GTC Taipei 2026, the achievement marks a significant milestone for China's embodied intelligence sector.**

item.title: From Shanghai to the World: UCloud and China's AI-Driven Cloud Export
item.description: On June 2, Shanghai-headquartered UCloud cut the ribbon on a new cloud computing node in Uzbekistan, pushing its global network to 36 nodes across 28 regions. The move might once have been a routine infrastructure announcement. In 2025, it is some...

item.title: Issue with Bot Management Traffic Categorization For Verified Bots
item.description: Jun 4, 00:12 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 4, 00:07 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 3, 22:36 UTC Identified - The cause of this issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 3, 22:31 UTC Investigating -...

item.title: Audit Log Delays
item.description: Jun 3, 21:25 UTC Resolved - An effective fix has been implemented and this incident is now resolved. Jun 3, 21:15 UTC Identified - The cause of this issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 3, 19:59 UTC Update - We are continuing to investigate this issue. Jun 3, 19:58 UTC...

item.title: 🔬Scaling Past Informal AI - Carina Hong, Axiom Math
item.description: Verified Generation and Compounding Intelligence

item.title: WhatsApp, Slack Notifications Could Hijack Google Gemini on Android
item.description: A single poisoned notification from WhatsApp, Slack, SMS, Signal, Instagram, or Messenger could have hijacked Google Gemini's voice assistant on Android and made it open a victim's connected windows, fake a message from their boss, push the phone into a Zoom call, or quietly poison its long-term...

item.title: Coralogix Raises $200M at $1.6B Valuation to Scale AI Observability Platform
item.description: Coralogix offers a full-stack observability platform that unifies logs, metrics, traces, security, and AI observability. The post Coralogix Raises $200M at $1.6B Valuation to Scale AI Observability Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ⚡️Satya Nadella: No Priors x Latent Space Crossover Special at Microsoft Build
item.description: The legendary Microsoft CEO makes his first Latent Space appearance!

item.title: How virtual power plants could provide energy for data centers
item.description: Would you take a payment to ramp down your electricity use? Would it change anything if you were doing so to help power a local data center? Google just signed a new deal to help pay for a virtual power plant (VPP) in the largest power grid in the US. The agreement is with Voltus,…

item.title: Customers are facing an increase in API errors when requesting Audit Logs
item.description: Jun 3, 14:29 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 3, 13:30 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Kirki, Burst Statistics WordPress Plugin Flaws in Attackers’ Crosshairs
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting vulnerable Kirki and Burst Statistics deployments to elevate privileges and take over websites. The post Kirki, Burst Statistics WordPress Plugin Flaws in Attackers’ Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Direct Preference Optimization Beyond Chatbots
The Download: Trump’s new AI order, and smart glasses for warfare
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. 5 key points in Trump’s new AI order Less than two weeks after scrapping an executive order on AI, President Donald Trump signed a new one on...

item.title: Elevated error rates on Codex, ChatGPT and Responses API
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational) Responses (Operational) Codex Web (Operational) App (Operational) Login (Operational)

item.title: Elevated errors on Opus 4.7
item.description: Jun 3, 07:38 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 3, 07:28 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 3, 07:10 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Issue affecting some Claude Code services
item.description: Jun 3, 07:36 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 3, 04:17 UTC Investigating - We have identified an issue which is resulting in some Claude Code services being degraded. As a result of this issue, users will see issues with Claude Code security reviews, code reviews, routines and...

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Jun 3, 06:46 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available. Jun 3, 06:45 UTC Monitoring - The degradation has been mitigated. We are monitoring to...

item.title: [AINews] Microsoft Build: MAI-Thinking-1 and MAI Family models
item.description: Microsoft Build recap, and new MAI model technical details

item.title: Learning What to Learn: Stage-Specific Data Sets for SFT-then-RL in Small Language Model Reasoning
item.description: Post-training Small Language Models (SLMs) for reasoning typically follows an SFT-then-RL pipeline, yet existing work rarely considers what data should be learned at each stage. We argue that data strategy should be aligned with the distinct roles of SFT and RL: SFT is better suited for acquiring...
